signals.headline: Cooper Flagg, Dallas Mavericks rookie, just dropped 45 points in a 134-128 win over the Los Angeles Lakers, achieving ba
signals.raw: Cooper Flagg, Dallas Mavericks rookie, just dropped 45 points in a 134-128 win over the Los Angeles Lakers, achieving back-to-back 40+ point games as the first teenager in NBA history and surpassing LeBron James for most 40-point games by a teen (4 total); he's now tied with Wilt Chamberlain for most points (96) by a rookie in a two-game span.**[1]
matched_markets: Will Cooper Flagg win the 2025–26 NBA Rookie of the Year award?
